# Lineara Roadmap

*Generated by pm-lineara on 2026-05-05 09:00 (UTC+1) — morning sweep (silent: no digest mail). Manual edits will be overwritten on next sweep — push back via OVERRIDE mail to pm-lineara, or by editing `~/.pogo/agents/pm/lineara.toml`.*

## Now (in flight)

- **mg-46d5** — v22: Minoan inferred-context external-validation comparison. Younger-catalog scholar-proposed Linear A readings vs framework mechanical per-inscription consensus. Extends v19's external-validation footprint from 1 entry (PS Za 2 libation formula) to ≥25 entries (libation formulae, accountancy markers, theonyms, place-names). Aggregate match-rate result will firm up or refine the "internal consensus ≠ external correctness" framing v20 made load-bearing. Just filed; awaiting dispatch. Budget 600k.

## Next (queued, available)

- **v23: Full cross-LM matrix for Eteocretan** — Eteocretan substrate candidates re-scored under Mycenaean Greek LM, Etruscan LM, plus the existing Aquitanian / Etruscan / toponym candidates re-scored under Eteocretan LM. Substantive negative-control battery; budget ~700k. v21 shipped a partial cross-LM (Eteocretan under Basque only) to keep scope tight; v23 closes the matrix.
- **v24: Per-inscription cascade-candidate analysis under Eteocretan LM** — analogous to v19's run on Aquitanian/Etruscan/toponym pools but with Eteocretan-shaped substrate signal. The strongest-pool result (Eteocretan p=4.10e-06) makes per-inscription consensus more likely to surface than under weaker pools; budget ~500k.
- **v25 (or later): Methodology paper polish pass after v22 + v23 + v24 land** — integrate Minoan-inferred external-validation aggregate, full cross-LM matrix, Eteocretan cascade candidates into `docs/findings_summary.md` cleanly; budget ~600k.

## Later (proposed)

- **Eteocretan bilingual decoding** (using Praisos / Dreros partial Greek translations to validate proposed Linear A readings). Methodologically distinct from current framework (would require v19-style external-validation framework reuse on a new population). Genuinely interesting; deferred until current substrate-validation cycle completes. Budget ~800k.
- **Per-window deduplication at generation time** (mg-f419 generator artifact). Pure compute-saving polish; deferred indefinitely.
- **Linear-B small-K refined gate adoption** as runner default. Documentation-level change; deferred.
- **Phoenician / Sumerian / Hattic substrate pools** — v15 settled the methodological limit (no per-sign decipherment). Adding more pools doesn't address that; would only multiply substrate-LM-phonotactic-kinship data points. Defer indefinitely.

## Backlog (open but no near-term plan)

- **mg-1b7c** — shelved as `dispatched`. Daniel-flagged future-corpus avenues; dispatched into v21 (Eteocretan, merged) and v22 (Minoan-inferred, in flight).

## Recently shipped (last 24h)

- **mg-6ccd (v21)** — Eteocretan corpus ingest + own-LM right-tail bayesian gate. **Strongest pool PASS in the validation series:** p=4.10e-06, posterior-median gap +0.20. Cross-LM (under Basque) still passes but ~600× weaker (p=2.58e-03), confirming substrate-LM-specificity. Gap-magnitude ordering across the 4 validated pools is now monotonic in genealogical relatedness: Eteocretan > toponym > Etruscan > Aquitanian. Substrate-detection methodology corroborated.
- **mg-711c (v20)** — methodology paper integration of v19 cascade-candidate findings + KU-RO/KI-RO scholarly-proposal investigation. Elevated the v19 PS Za 2 external-validation result from "minimal paragraph" to load-bearing-finding status across Abstract / §3 / §4 / §5 / §6. KU-RO and KI-RO null on KH 10 / KH 5 (sequences absent).
- **mg-2bfd (v17)** — restored mg-9e00 + mg-1c8c lineage citations in findings_summary.md (v16 polecat error); audit-of-the-audit confirmed v16's other two drift fixes were correct.
- **mg-d5ed (v16)** — first full audit-and-polish pass of `docs/findings_summary.md`; methodology paper draft now publication-readable.
- **mg-9f18 (v18)** — toponym pool fix (bigram-preserving control sampler, p=9.99e-05 PASS) + pollution-level sweep (10/25/50/75% Aquitanian, gate stays PASS at all four levels).
- **mg-7ecb (v15)** — cross-language pollution test. Greek-shape conjecturals in Aquitanian pool: gate PASSes weakly (p=2.006e-03), within-tail real-vs-Greek-shape MW p=8.29e-05 (real shape selectivity is detectable, just below the population-gate level).
- **mg-6b73 (v14)** — same-distribution pollution test. Polluted Aquitanian (50% real / 50% conjectural) PASSes at v10 magnitude; framework cannot distinguish real from conjectural within mixed pool — phonotactic shape is the discriminator, not provenance.
- **mg-c216 (v13)** — per-sign cross-window coherence test FAILED decisively (median 0.18 vs 0.6 acceptance bar). v10 PASSes don't underwrite per-sign decipherment.
- **mg-3438 (v19)** — per-inscription cascade-decipherment investigation. 3 cascade candidates emerged (KH 10, KH 5, PS Za 2). PS Za 2 mechanical reading on libation-formula span (`th-u-u-n-i`) DIVERGES from scholarly `ja-sa-sa-ra-me` on all 5 consonantal positions — first external-validation negative result; strongest discipline-protecting finding in the project.

## Trajectory

**26 lineara tickets shipped** (mg-9e00 scaffold + mg-1c8c corpus + mg-d5ef through mg-7ecb harness lineage v0-v15 + mg-d5ed/mg-2bfd/mg-9f18/mg-3438/mg-711c/mg-6ccd polish/extension v16-v21 + mg-13a2 findings.md scaffolding). 1 ticket in flight (v22). 1 shelved as dispatched (mg-1b7c).

**Methodology paper status:** publication-readable. Three external-validation pillars now in place (1: per-surface aggregate failure on coherence; 2: per-inscription PS Za 2 vs scholarly libation-formula divergence; 3: substrate-LM-phonotactic-kinship signal scales monotonically with genealogical relatedness across 4 pools). v22 will add a 4th pillar (broader scholarly-proposal comparison set).

**Supportable claim, current form:** the framework reliably detects substrate-LM-phonotactic kinship at population level for any pool whose phoneme + length distribution overlaps the substrate-LM's expectations; the strength of detection scales monotonically with a-priori genealogical relatedness (Eteocretan strongest, Aquitanian weakest); within the right tail it discriminates real-substrate-shape from cross-language-shape; it does NOT support per-sign decipherment claims at the per-surface aggregate level (v13) NOR at the per-inscription external-validation level (v19/v20); the cascade-candidate framing — find inscriptions with high internal consensus, validate against external scholarly ground truth — is itself a methodological contribution other research groups could adopt.

**What remains for journal submission (out of polecat scope):** LaTeX formatting, target-venue-specific narrative tightening, peer review, domain-expert review of top-K substrate surfaces (especially Eteocretan top-K given v21's strong PASS).
